text: Add src/cli/UpdateRequestCommand.js to implement the thin command-line update flow on top of the update client.
goal: Implement apiease read, apiease update, and apiease delete as public CLI commands.
context: The existing RequestDefinitionFileLoader already provides the CLI-side request JSON loading behavior used by create.
Update should follow the same API key resolution and output-mode conventions as the existing create command.
steps: Parse the update command arguments for request id, request definition file path, API base URL, shop domain, optional API key, and optional JSON output.
Load the updated request definition through RequestDefinitionFileLoader and resolve the API key through ApiEaseHomeConfigurationResolver when needed.
Call the update client and render human-readable or JSON output with the correct exit code.
constraints: Keep request file parsing in the existing loader instead of duplicating JSON file handling in the command.
Do not embed contract validation or HTTP transport logic in src/cli.
acceptanceCriteria: The command supports apiease update --request-id <id> --file <path> --base-url <url> --shop-domain <shop-domain> with optional --api-key <api-key> and --json.
File loading failures, API key resolution failures, validation failures, and backend failures are surfaced through the existing structured CLI result pattern.
Default output clearly reports a successful request update and JSON mode emits the raw structured result.
Success exits zero and failures exit non-zero.
